sentence: O tempo não está bom hoje, não vou sair.
ipa: u ˈtẽpu ˈnɐ̃w̃ eˈʃta ˈbõ ˈoʒi ˈnɐ̃w̃ ˈvow ˈsajh
gloss_en: The weather is not good today, I'm not going out.
notes: Coastal-Fluminense coda /S/ palatalised 'está' [eˈʃta] and word-final coda /r/ aspirated 'sair' [ˈsajh] per callou2010 and silva2002.

sentence: Essas duas camisas custam trinta reais.
ipa: ˈesaʃ ˈduaʃ kaˈmizaʃ ˈkuʃtɐ̃w̃ ˈtɾĩtɐ xeˈajʃ
gloss_en: These two shirts cost thirty reais.
notes: Coda [ʃ] 'camisas'/'reais' and velar onset /r/ 'reais' [xeˈajʃ] per callou2010 and silva2002.

sentence: A consulta é amanhã às três da tarde.
ipa: ɐ kõˈsuwtɐ ˈɛ amaˈɲɐ̃ aʃ ˈtɾeʃ dɐ ˈtaxd͡ʒi
gloss_en: The appointment is tomorrow at three in the afternoon.
notes: Palatal [ɲ] 'amanhã', chiado 'às três' and velar coda /r/ 'tarde' [ˈtaxdi] per callou2010 and mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Meu irmão mora perto daqui com a família.
ipa: ˈmew iˈxmɐ̃w̃ ˈmɔɾɐ ˈpextu daˈki kõ ɐ faˈmiliɐ
gloss_en: My brother lives near here with the family.
notes: Nasal diphthong 'irmão' and velar coda /r/ 'perto' [ˈpextu] per callou2010 and callou_leite2001.

sentence: Vira à direita e segue reto até a praça.
ipa: ˈviɾɐ a d͡ʒiˈɾejtɐ i ˈseɡi ˈxetu aˈtɛ ɐ ˈpɾasɐ
gloss_en: Turn right and keep straight on until the square.
notes: Affrication 'direita' and velar onset /r/ 'reto' [ˈxetu] per silva2002 and callou2010.

sentence: A comida daquele restaurante estava deliciosa.
ipa: ɐ koˈmidɐ daˈkeli xeʃtaˈwɾɐ̃t͡ʃi eˈʃtavɐ delis̺iˈozɐ
gloss_en: The food at that restaurant was delicious.
notes: Velar onset /r/ 'restaurante' [x] and chiado 'estava' [eˈʃtavɐ] per callou2010 and silva2002.

sentence: Onde é que a gente pega o ônibus pro centro?
ipa: ˈõd͡ʒi ˈɛ ki ɐ ˈʒẽt͡ʃi ˈpeɡɐ u ˈonibuʃ ˈpɾu ˈs̺ẽtɾu
gloss_en: Where do we catch the bus to downtown?
notes: Colloquial 'a gente'/'ônibus'; coda [ʃ] 'ônibus' [ˈonibuʃ] and open-mid [ɛ] per callou2010 and mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Quantas pessoas você vai levar na viagem?
ipa: kwɐ̃ˈtaʃ peˈsoaʃ voˈs̺e ˈvaj leˈvah nɐ viˈaʒẽj̃
gloss_en: How many people are you taking on the trip?
notes: 'você' with 3sg; word-final coda /r/ aspirated 'levar' [leˈvah] and coda [ʃ] per callou2010 and silva2002.

sentence: Se eu pudesse, comprava aquela casa azul.
ipa: si ˈew puˈdesi kõˈpɾavɐ aˈkelɐ ˈkazɐ aˈzuw
gloss_en: If I could, I would buy that blue house.
notes: Coda /l/ vocalised 'azul' [aˈzuw] and final raising 'pudesse' per silva2002 and callou_leite2001.

sentence: Ontem eu comprei frutas e verduras na feira.
ipa: ˈõtẽj̃ ˈew kõˈpɾej ˈfɾutaʃ i veˈxduɾaʃ nɐ ˈfejɾɐ
gloss_en: Yesterday I bought fruit and vegetables at the market.
notes: 'ei' 'feira', velar coda /r/ 'verduras' [veˈxduɾaʃ] and chiado 'frutas' per callou2010 and silva2002.

sentence: Semana que vem a gente vai viajar de trem.
ipa: seˈmanɐ ki ˈvẽj̃ ɐ ˈʒẽt͡ʃi ˈvaj viaˈʒah d͡ʒi ˈtɾẽj̃
gloss_en: Next week we're going to travel by train.
notes: 'a gente'/'trem'; nasal [ẽ] and word-final coda /r/ aspirated 'viajar' [viaˈʒah] per callou2010 and silva2002.

sentence: Me liga quando você chegar em casa, tá legal?
ipa: mi ˈliɡɐ ˈkwɐ̃du voˈs̺e ʃeˈɡah ẽj̃ ˈkazɐ ˈta leˈɡaw
gloss_en: Call me when you get home, all right?
notes: Imperative 'me liga'; word-final coda /r/ aspirated 'chegar' [ʃeˈɡah] and coda /l/ vocalised 'legal' per callou2010 and silva2002.

sentence: Bom dia, tudo bem com você hoje?
ipa: ˈbõ ˈd͡ʒiɐ ˈtudu ˈbẽj̃ kõ voˈs̺e ˈoʒi
gloss_en: Good morning, how are you today?
notes: 'você' greeting; affrication 'dia' [ˈd͡ʒiɐ] and nasal [õ]/[ẽ] per silva2002 and mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Quem não arrisca não petisca.
ipa: ˈkẽj̃ ˈnɐ̃w̃ aˈʁiʃkɐ ˈnɐ̃w̃ peˈt͡ʃiʃkɐ
gloss_en: Nothing ventured, nothing gained.
notes: Chiado codas 'arrisca'/'petisca' [aˈʁiʃkɐ peˈt͡ʃiʃkɐ] and affrication before [i] per callou2010 and silva2002.

sentence: O gato preto estava dormindo no sofá da sala.
ipa: u ˈɡatu ˈpɾetu eˈʃtavɐ doˈxmĩdu nu soˈfa dɐ ˈsalɐ
gloss_en: The black cat was sleeping on the living-room sofa.
notes: 'gato' [ˈɡatu] final raising, chiado 'estava' and estar+gerúndio 'dormindo' per callou2010 and silva2002.

sentence: Aquele rato correu pro buraco do muro.
ipa: aˈkeli ˈxatu koˈʁew ˈpɾu buˈɾaku du ˈmuɾu
gloss_en: That mouse ran into the hole in the wall.
notes: 'rato' [ˈxatu] velar initial /r/ and final raising 'muro' per callou2010 and silva2002.

sentence: O mundo inteiro fala desse assunto agora.
ipa: u ˈmũdu ĩˈtejɾu ˈfalɐ ˈdesi aˈsũtu aˈɡoɾɐ
gloss_en: The whole world is talking about this subject now.
notes: Nasal 'mundo' [ˈmũdu] and 'ei' 'inteiro'; 'mundo inteiro' junction per mateus_dandrade2000 and silva2002.

sentence: Ele é muito chato quando bebe demais.
ipa: ˈeli ˈɛ ˈmujtu ˈʃatu ˈkwɐ̃du ˈbebi deˈmajʃ
gloss_en: He is very annoying when he drinks too much.
notes: Lexical [ʃ] 'chato', word-final coda [ʃ] 'demais' [deˈmajʃ] and open-mid [ɛ] per callou2010 and mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: A tia trouxe leite e pão doce de tarde.
ipa: ɐ ˈt͡ʃiɐ ˈtɾowsi ˈlejt͡ʃi i ˈpɐ̃w̃ ˈdos̺i d͡ʒi ˈtaxd͡ʒi
gloss_en: Auntie brought milk and sweet bread in the afternoon.
notes: Affrication 'tia' [ˈt͡ʃiɐ], nasal diphthong 'pão' and velar coda /r/ 'tarde' [ˈtaxdi] per silva2002 and callou2010.

sentence: O sol brilha forte por cima dos telhados.
ipa: u ˈsɔw ˈbɾiʎɐ ˈfɔxt͡ʃi poh ˈs̺imɐ doʃ teˈʎadoʃ
gloss_en: The sun shines strongly over the rooftops.
notes: Coda /l/ vocalised 'sol' [ˈsow], palatal [ʎ] 'brilha', chiado 'dos telhados' and velar coda /r/ 'forte' [ˈfoxti] per callou2010 and cardoso_etal2014.
